# Phase 8: Growth & Retention ‚Äî Implementation Plan

**Goal:** Transform Local Roots from a functional marketplace into a growth-ready platform with buyer retention loops, seller engagement analytics, organic acquisition via SEO, and hardened payout infrastructure.

**North Star:** Increase completed pickups per week by building the systems that keep buyers coming back and help sellers see the value of the platform.

**Architecture:** Go backend (new endpoints + cron jobs), Next.js App Router frontend (new pages + enhanced components), PostgreSQL (new queries, no schema changes expected), Resend (new email templates).

---

## Design Specifications (added by design review)

### Design Tokens (from globals.css ‚Äî no new tokens needed)
- `--lr-bg` (#f6f1e8 sand) ‚Äî page backgrounds, OG image background
- `--lr-ink` (#1c1b16 ink) ‚Äî primary text, metric values
- `--lr-muted` (#4a463c peat) ‚Äî labels, secondary text, timestamps
- `--lr-leaf` (#2f6b4f sage) ‚Äî positive indicators, CTAs, filled stars, trend ‚ñ≤
- `--lr-clay` (#b35d2e clay) ‚Äî warning/error indicators, trend ‚ñº, "Action needed"
- `lr-card` / `lr-card-strong` ‚Äî standard (78%) / emphasized (92%) card opacity
- `lr-chip` ‚Äî pill badges for status and cadence

### A11y: Bump `lr-btn-primary` touch targets to `py-2.5` (44px min) in globals.css.

### Analytics Page ‚Äî Two-Tier Metric Layout
```
PRIMARY ROW (4 cards, lr-card-strong, 2xl values):
  Active Subs (‚ñ≤/‚ñº delta vs 4 weeks ago) | Pickup Rate | Revenue (‚ñ≤/‚ñº growth %) | Avg Rating (‚òÖ stars)

SECONDARY ROW (3 cards, lr-card, xl values, muted):
  Retention Rate | No-Show Rate | Churn

TOP PRODUCTS (ranked list section, not chart):
  1. Product title ‚Äî qty sold ‚Äî revenue

EXISTING SECTIONS (unchanged): Revenue by Cycle table, Payout History table
```
- Subscriber trend: ‚ñ≤/‚ñº arrow with delta vs 4 weeks ago on Active Subs card
- Avg Rating: reuse ReviewSummary star component, show "No reviews yet" when null
- All trend arrows: `aria-label="Up 3 from 4 weeks ago"` for screen readers
- Empty state (new store): "Your store is brand new ‚Äî metrics appear after your first pickup"

### "What's in the Box" ‚Äî Buyer Display
- Placed on store detail page **above** "What's available" section
- Single `lr-card-strong` with optional photo left (200px), title + body right
- "Posted X days ago" timestamp in `--lr-muted`
- If no box update exists: section doesn't render (not an empty state)
- Mobile: photo stacks on top, text below
- Photo `alt`: store name + "box contents"

### "What's in the Box" ‚Äî Seller Posting UI
- Inline on seller dashboard, near upcoming pickup (not in settings)
- Empty prompt: "Share what's in this week's box with your subscribers"
- Form: Title (single line), Body (textarea), Photo (optional, reuse `ImageUpload`)
- Post/Cancel buttons, loading state "Posting‚Ä¶" with disabled button
- Success: toast "Update posted!" + card appears
- Error: `ErrorAlert` with retry

### City Landing Page (`/farms/{city-region}`)
- h1: "Farm Boxes in {City}, {Region}"
- Subtitle: "Fresh local food, picked up weekly from farms near you."
- Reuse existing `StoreCard` in `sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3` grid
- Empty state: warm copy + waitlist email capture (reuse stores page pattern)
- Breadcrumb: Farms ‚Üí {City}, {Region}

### Waitlist Form (stores page)
- Already partially built (notifyEmail/notifySubmitted state exists)
- Enhancement: submit geo coords (lat/lng from search context) to `POST /v1/waitlist`
- Success: "You're on the list!" with green checkmark (existing pattern)
- Duplicate: "You're already on our list"

### Payout Status Chips (4 states)
- Transferred: `--lr-leaf` bg/10, leaf text (existing)
- Processing: amber bg, amber text (new)
- Retrying: `--lr-clay` bg/10, clay text (new)
- Action needed: `--lr-clay` bg/10, clay text, bold (new)

### OG Image (`/stores/{storeId}/opengraph-image`)
- Background: `--lr-bg` sand
- Left accent bar: `--lr-leaf` sage (4px)
- Text: `--lr-ink` ‚Äî store name (large), price range, review stars if available
- No gradients, no stock photos ‚Äî branded fallback when no hero image
- Built with Next.js `ImageResponse` API

### Responsive Breakpoints
| Surface | Desktop | Tablet | Mobile |
|---------|---------|--------|--------|
| Analytics primary row | `md:grid-cols-4` | `grid-cols-2` | `grid-cols-2` |
| Analytics secondary row | `grid-cols-3` | `grid-cols-3` | `grid-cols-1` |
| Box update card (buyer) | Image left, text right | Same | Image top, text bottom |
| City landing grid | `lg:grid-cols-3` | `sm:grid-cols-2` | `grid-cols-1` |
| Location picker | Radio inline | Same | Stack vertical |

### Interaction State Coverage
| Feature | Loading | Empty | Error | Success |
|---------|---------|-------|-------|---------|
| Analytics (enhanced) | "Loading analytics‚Ä¶" | "Brand new ‚Äî metrics after first pickup" | Toast | Two-tier cards |
| Box update (buyer) | N/A (SSR) | Section hidden | Graceful degradation | Card renders |
| Box update (seller) | "Posting‚Ä¶" disabled | "Share what's in this week's box" | ErrorAlert | Toast + card |
| Waitlist capture | "Joining‚Ä¶" disabled | N/A (shows on 0 results) | Inline error | "You're on the list!" |
| City page | Skeleton cards (3) | Warm copy + waitlist | "Couldn't load" + retry | Store grid |
| Top products | Part of page load | "No orders yet" | Part of analytics | Ranked list |
| Payout status | Existing table | Existing | Existing | 4-state chips |
| Multi-location mgmt | "Saving‚Ä¶" | "Add first pickup location" | ErrorAlert | Toast |

---

## Overview: What Exists vs. What's New

| Area | Already Built | Phase 8 Adds |
|------|--------------|-------------|
| **Payouts** | Auto-transfer on pickup/no-show, payout history, summary per window | Transfer failure recovery, retry mechanism, aggregated payout dashboard, expected next payout |
| **Analytics** | Subscriber count, revenue by cycle, pickup rate, payout history | Trend sparklines, review score integration, retention rate, subscriber cohort metrics, "last active" tracking |
| **Re-engagement** | 11 transactional emails, pickup reminders, billing cron | Lapsed subscriber detection, win-back emails, post-pickup review prompt, subscription renewal nudges |
| **Multi-location** | 1:N store‚Üípickup_locations schema, geo search | Seller multi-location management UI, buyer location picker on subscription checkout |
| **SEO / Profiles** | Store detail page with generateMetadata, basic OG tags | Rich public profiles, city landing pages, structured data (JSON-LD), sitemap.xml, robots.txt, OG images |

---

## Priority Order

1. **Buyer Re-engagement** ‚Äî Directly impacts retention (the #1 metric). Zero infrastructure exists.
2. **Enhanced Seller Analytics** ‚Äî Keeps sellers engaged, shows them value of platform.
3. **Public Store Profiles / SEO** ‚Äî Unlocks organic buyer acquisition channel.
4. **Payout Hardening** ‚Äî Important but not blocking (transfers already work).
5. **Multi-location UX** ‚Äî Lowest urgency (schema exists, few farmers need it at early stage).

---

## Task 1: Buyer Re-engagement ‚Äî Lapsed Subscriber Detection

**Files:**
- Create: `backend/internal/api/v1/internal_reengagement.go`
- Modify: `backend/internal/scheduler/scheduler.go` (add cron job)
- Modify: `backend/internal/email/templates.go` (add 3 new templates)
- Modify: `backend/internal/httpx/handler.go` (register internal endpoint)

### Step 1: Define "lapsed" criteria

A subscriber is considered lapsed when:
- Status is `active` (not paused or canceled)
- Their last picked-up order was 2+ cycles ago (based on plan cadence)
- They have not been sent a re-engagement email in the last 14 days

### Step 2: Add re-engagement tracking column

Create migration `backend/migrations/XXXX_reengagement_tracking.sql`:
```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE subscriptions ADD COLUMN last_reengagement_email_at TIMESTAMPTZ;

-- +goose Down
ALTER TABLE subscriptions DROP COLUMN last_reengagement_email_at;
```

### Step 3: Build lapsed subscriber query

```sql
SELECT s.id, s.buyer_email, s.buyer_name, sp.title as plan_title,
       sp.cadence, st.name as store_name, st.id as store_id
FROM subscriptions s
JOIN subscription_plans sp ON sp.id = s.plan_id
JOIN stores st ON st.id = s.store_id
WHERE s.status = 'active'
  AND (s.last_reengagement_email_at IS NULL
       OR s.last_reengagement_email_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '14 days')
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM orders o
    WHERE o.subscription_id = s.id
      AND o.status = 'picked_up'
      AND o.picked_up_at > NOW() - (
        CASE sp.cadence
          WHEN 'weekly' THEN INTERVAL '14 days'
          WHEN 'biweekly' THEN INTERVAL '28 days'
          WHEN 'monthly' THEN INTERVAL '60 days'
        END
      )
  )
LIMIT 50;
```

### Step 4: Add email templates

Three new templates in `templates.go`:
1. **LapsedSubscriberNudge** ‚Äî "We miss you at {store_name}! Your next {plan_title} box is waiting."
2. **PostPickupReviewPrompt** ‚Äî "How was your box from {store_name}?" (sent 2 hours after pickup confirmation)
3. **UpcomingPickupExcitement** ‚Äî "Your {plan_title} box is ready tomorrow! Here's what to expect." (enhancement of existing reminder with richer content)

### Step 5: Build cron job

Add `RunReengagement()` to scheduler, running every 6 hours:
- Query lapsed subscribers (limit 50 per run to avoid email bursts)
- Send `LapsedSubscriberNudge` email
- Update `last_reengagement_email_at`

### Step 6: Add post-pickup review prompt

In `pickup_execute.go`, after the pickup confirmation + payment receipt email, schedule a delayed review prompt:
- Add `review_prompt_sent_at` column to orders (migration)
- Cron job checks for orders picked up 2+ hours ago with no review and no prompt sent
- Sends `PostPickupReviewPrompt` email with link to review page

### Step 7: Tests

- Test lapsed query logic with mock data (active sub, no recent pickups)
- Test email template rendering
- Test cron idempotency (re-running doesn't re-send)
- Test edge cases: paused subs excluded, canceled subs excluded, recently engaged excluded

### Step 8: Verification

```bash
cd backend && go test ./...
```

---

## Task 2: Enhanced Seller Analytics

**Files:**
- Modify: `backend/internal/api/v1/seller_analytics.go` (enhance response)
- Modify: `frontend/src/app/seller/stores/[storeId]/analytics/page.tsx` (add visualizations)
- Modify: `frontend/src/lib/seller-api.ts` (update types)

### Step 1: Enhance analytics endpoint response

Add to `AnalyticsResponse`:
```go
type AnalyticsResponse struct {
    // ... existing fields ...

    // New fields
    RetentionRate      float64              `json:"retention_rate"`       // subscribers retained over last 30 days
    AvgRating          *float64             `json:"avg_rating"`           // average review score (nullable)
    ReviewCount        int                  `json:"review_count"`         // total reviews
    SubscriberTrend    []TrendPoint         `json:"subscriber_trend"`     // last 12 weeks subscriber count snapshots
    RevenueGrowthPct   *float64             `json:"revenue_growth_pct"`   // revenue change vs prior period
    TopProducts        []TopProduct         `json:"top_products"`         // top 5 ordered products
    NoShowRate         float64              `json:"no_show_rate"`         // no_show / total non-canceled
}

type TrendPoint struct {
    Date  string `json:"date"`
    Value int    `json:"value"`
}

type TopProduct struct {
    Title    string `json:"title"`
    Quantity int    `json:"quantity"`
    Revenue  int    `json:"revenue_cents"`
}
```

### Step 2: Add review aggregation query

```sql
SELECT COALESCE(AVG(r.rating), 0), COUNT(r.id)
FROM reviews r WHERE r.store_id = $1;
```

### Step 3: Add retention rate calculation

```sql
-- Subscribers who were active 30 days ago and are still active
WITH cohort AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as total
    FROM subscriptions
    WHERE store_id = $1 AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
      AND status != 'canceled'
)
SELECT
    CASE WHEN cohort.total = 0 THEN 0
    ELSE (
        SELECT COUNT(*)::float / cohort.total
        FROM subscriptions
        WHERE store_id = $1 AND created_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
          AND status = 'active'
    ) END as retention_rate
FROM cohort;
```

### Step 4: Add top products query

```sql
SELECT oi.product_title, SUM(oi.quantity) as total_qty,
       SUM(oi.price_cents * oi.quantity) as total_revenue
FROM order_items oi
JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id
WHERE o.store_id = $1 AND o.status = 'picked_up'
GROUP BY oi.product_title
ORDER BY total_qty DESC
LIMIT 5;
```

### Step 5: Frontend ‚Äî Enhance analytics page

Update the analytics page to show:
1. **Metric cards row** ‚Äî Active subscribers (with trend arrow), Pickup rate, Avg rating (stars), Revenue (with growth %)
2. **Revenue by cycle** ‚Äî Keep existing table, add visual sparkline
3. **Retention rate** ‚Äî Simple gauge or percentage display
4. **Top products** ‚Äî Small ranked list
5. **No-show rate** ‚Äî Percentage with context

### Step 6: Tests

- Test new analytics queries return correct aggregations
- Test edge cases: no reviews (null avg), no orders, new store with zero data
- Frontend: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint`

### Step 7: Verification

```bash
cd backend && go test ./...
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint
```

## Task 3: Public Store Profiles & SEO Infrastructure

**Files:**

- Create: `frontend/src/app/sitemap.ts` (dynamic sitemap)

- Modify: `frontend/src/app/stores/page.tsx` (enhanced meta)
- Modify: `backend/internal/api/v1/public.go` (add cities endpoint)

### Step 1: Add public cities endpoint

```go
// GET /v1/cities ‚Äî returns distinct cities with active stores
// Response: [{ "city": "Austin", "region": "TX", "store_count": 5 }]
```

### Step 2: Enhance store detail page with structured data

Add JSON-LD to the store detail page:
```typescript
const jsonLd = {
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LocalBusiness",
  "name": store.name,
  "description": store.description,
  "address": { "@type": "PostalAddress", ... },
  "geo": { "@type": "GeoCoordinates", ... },
  "aggregateRating": reviews.length > 0 ? {
    "@type": "AggregateRating",
    "ratingValue": avgRating,
    "reviewCount": reviews.length
  } : undefined
};
```

### Step 3: Create city landing pages

`/farms/[city]` ‚Äî server-rendered pages showing farms in a city:
- Dynamic metadata: "Farm Boxes in {City}, {Region} ‚Äî Local Roots"
- Lists stores with live plans in that city
- Structured data for the collection
- Links to individual store pages

### Step 4: Create dynamic sitemap

`frontend/src/app/sitemap.ts`:
```typescript
export default async function sitemap(): Promise<MetadataRoute.Sitemap> {
  const stores = await api.listStores();
  const cities = await api.listCities();
  return [
    { url: 'https://localroots.com', changeFrequency: 'weekly', priority: 1 },
    { url: 'https://localroots.com/stores', changeFrequency: 'daily', priority: 0.9 },
    ...cities.map(c => ({
      url: `https://localroots.com/farms/${c.city.toLowerCase()}`,
      changeFrequency: 'weekly' as const,
      priority: 0.8,
    })),
    ...stores.map(s => ({
      url: `https://localroots.com/stores/${s.id}`,
      changeFrequency: 'weekly' as const,
      priority: 0.7,
    })),
  ];
}
```

### Step 5: Create robots.ts

```typescript
export default function robots(): MetadataRoute.Robots {
  return {
    rules: { userAgent: '*', allow: '/', disallow: ['/seller/', '/buyer/', '/auth/'] },
    sitemap: 'https://localroots.com/sitemap.xml',
  };
}
```

### Step 6: Enhance store cards with richer data

## Task 4: Payout Hardening

**Files:**
- Modify: `backend/internal/api/v1/order_helpers.go` (add retry logic)
- Create: `backend/internal/api/v1/internal_transfer_retry.go` (retry cron)
- Modify: `backend/internal/scheduler/scheduler.go` (register retry job)
- Modify: `frontend/src/app/seller/stores/[storeId]/analytics/page.tsx` (payout status UI)

### Step 1: Track transfer failures

Add migration for transfer attempt tracking:
```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN transfer_attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ;
ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN transfer_error TEXT;

-- +goose Down
ALTER TABLE orders DROP COLUMN transfer_attempted_at;
ALTER TABLE orders DROP COLUMN transfer_error;
```

### Step 2: Update transferToSeller to record failures

Instead of silently no-oping on failure, record the attempt:
```go
func transferToSeller(...) error {
    // ... existing logic ...
    if err != nil {
        // Record failure for retry
        db.Exec(ctx, `UPDATE orders SET transfer_attempted_at = NOW(), transfer_error = $1 WHERE id = $2`,
            err.Error(), orderID)
        return err
    }
    // Record success
    db.Exec(ctx, `UPDATE orders SET stripe_transfer_id = $1, transfer_attempted_at = NOW(), transfer_error = NULL WHERE id = $2`,
        transfer.ID, orderID)
    return nil
}
```

### Step 3: Build retry cron job

`RunTransferRetry()` ‚Äî runs every hour:
- Finds orders with `transfer_attempted_at IS NOT NULL AND stripe_transfer_id IS NULL AND transfer_error IS NOT NULL`
- Retries transfer up to 3 times (check attempt count or age)
- After 3 failures, flag for manual intervention

### Step 4: Frontend ‚Äî Show transfer status more clearly

On the payout history page:
- "Transferred" (green) ‚Äî has transfer_id
- "Processing" (amber) ‚Äî no transfer_id, attempted < 1 hour ago
- "Failed ‚Äî Retrying" (red) ‚Äî has error, under retry limit
- "Action needed" (red) ‚Äî exceeded retry limit

### Step 5: Tests

- Test transfer failure recording
- Test retry logic doesn't double-transfer
- Test retry limit enforcement

# TODOS

Deferred work items tracked for future implementation.

---

## P2: "Next pickup" date badge on store cards

**What:** Show "Next pickup: Sat Mar 22" on each store card in `/stores` browse results and city landing pages.

**Why:** Growth audit flagged pickup date as a top purchase signal ‚Äî knowing *when* you can get food drives action. Currently buyers must click into a store to see pickup dates.

**Pros:** Surfaces purchase-intent data earlier in the funnel, reduces unnecessary click-throughs, increases qualified traffic to store detail pages.

**Cons:** Requires an additional query per store (next published pickup window) ‚Äî minor performance cost.

**Context:** Backend needs to include `next_pickup_date` in the store list response by querying upcoming published windows for each store. Frontend renders it as a badge on `StoreCard`. The `pickup_windows` table already has `start_at` and `status` columns. Could use a lateral join or subquery in the existing `ListStores` query.

**Effort:** S (human: ~4h / CC: ~10 min)

**Priority:** P2

**Depends on / blocked by:** None. Can be done independently of Phase 8.

**Source:** CEO plan review 2026-03-18, Phase 8 deferred item.

input.new_string:
**Source:** CEO plan review 2026-03-18, Phase 8 deferred item.

---

## P3: Formalize design system into DESIGN.md

**What:** Run `/design-consultation` to document the implicit design language (tokens, component classes, typography scale, spacing, color usage) into a formal `DESIGN.md` file.

**Why:** The design system is currently implicit ‚Äî spread across `globals.css` CSS variables and component patterns. As the codebase grows (Phase 8 adds 4+ new UI surfaces), design drift becomes likely without a single source of truth. New contributors won't know which tokens to use or which patterns to follow.

**Pros:** Prevents design drift, speeds up future UI work, enables design review automation against documented patterns, makes onboarding easier.

**Cons:** Maintenance overhead of keeping the doc in sync. Low risk since the design language is already stable.

**Context:** Existing tokens: `--lr-bg`, `--lr-ink`, `--lr-muted`, `--lr-leaf`, `--lr-clay`, `--lr-water`. Component classes: `lr-card`, `lr-card-strong`, `lr-btn`, `lr-btn-primary`, `lr-chip`, `lr-field`. Typography: 3xl page headings, base section headings, xs uppercase labels, sm body. Hover lift: `hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-[0_22px_60px_rgba(38,28,10,0.14)]`. The `/design-consultation` skill can analyze the codebase and produce the doc.

**Effort:** S (human: ~2h / CC: ~20 min)

**Priority:** P3

**Depends on / blocked by:** None. Can be done independently. Best done after Phase 8 ships so the doc captures all patterns.

**Source:** Design review 2026-03-21, Phase 8 plan.

`‚òÖ Insight ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ`

1‚Üípackage scheduler
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"log"
     6‚Üí	"time"
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí	v1 "github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/api/v1"
    11‚Üí	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/email"
    12‚Üí	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/payments/stripepay"
    13‚Üí)
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí// Start launches in-process cron jobs. It blocks until ctx is cancelled.
    16‚Üí// Call this in a goroutine from main.go.
    17‚Üí//
    18‚Üí// Jobs:
    19‚Üí//   - Billing authorization: every 30 minutes
    20‚Üí//   - Pickup reminders: every 30 minutes (offset by 15 min)
    21‚Üífunc Start(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, stripeClient *stripepay.Client, emailClient *email.Client, frontendURL string) {
    22‚Üí	billingTicker := time.NewTicker(30 * time.Minute)
    23‚Üí	reminderTicker := time.NewTicker(30 * time.Minute)
    24‚Üí	defer billingTicker.Stop()
    25‚Üí	defer reminderTicker.Stop()
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí	log.Printf("scheduler: started (billing=30m, reminders=30m)")
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí	// Run billing immediately on startup, then on tick.
    30‚Üí	go runBilling(ctx, db, stripeClient)
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí	// Delay reminders by 15 min to stagger with billing.
    33‚Üí	go func() {
    34‚Üí		select {
    35‚Üí		case <-time.After(15 * time.Minute):
    36‚Üí			runReminders(ctx, db, emailClient, frontendURL)
    37‚Üí		case <-ctx.Done():
    38‚Üí		}
    39‚Üí	}()
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí	for {
    42‚Üí		select {
    43‚Üí		case <-ctx.Done():
    44‚Üí			log.Printf("scheduler: stopped")
    45‚Üí			return
    46‚Üí		case <-billingTicker.C:
    47‚Üí			go runBilling(ctx, db, stripeClient)
    48‚Üí		case <-reminderTicker.C:
    49‚Üí			go runReminders(ctx, db, emailClient, frontendURL)
    50‚Üí		}
    51‚Üí	}
    52‚Üí}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üífunc runBilling(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, stripeClient *stripepay.Client) {
    55‚Üí	defer func() {
    56‚Üí		if r := recover(); r != nil {
    57‚Üí			log.Printf("scheduler: billing panic: %v", r)
    58‚Üí		}
    59‚Üí	}()
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí	if db == nil || stripeClient == nil || !stripeClient.Enabled() {
    62‚Üí		return
    63‚Üí	}
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí	result, err := v1.RunBillingAuthorization(ctx, db, stripeClient)
    66‚Üí	if err != nil {
    67‚Üí		log.Printf("scheduler: billing error: %v", err)
    68‚Üí		return
    69‚Üí	}
    70‚Üí	log.Printf("scheduler: billing candidates=%d authorized=%d failed=%d until=%s",
    71‚Üí		result.Candidates, result.Authorized, result.Failed, result.Until.Format(time.RFC3339))
    72‚Üí}
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üífunc runReminders(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, emailClient *email.Client, frontendURL string) {
    75‚Üí	defer func() {
    76‚Üí		if r := recover(); r != nil {
    77‚Üí			log.Printf("scheduler: reminders panic: %v", r)
    78‚Üí		}
    79‚Üí	}()
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí	if db == nil || emailClient == nil || !emailClient.Enabled() {
    82‚Üí		return
    83‚Üí	}
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí	result, err := v1.RunPickupReminders(ctx, db, emailClient, frontendURL)
    86‚Üí	if err != nil {
    87‚Üí		log.Printf("scheduler: reminders error: %v", err)
    88‚Üí		return
    89‚Üí	}
    90‚Üí	log.Printf("scheduler: reminders candidates=%d sent=%d",
    91‚Üí		result.Candidates, result.Sent)
    92‚Üí}
    93‚Üí

   202‚Üí	return
   203‚Üí}

1‚Üípackage v1
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"net/http"
     5‚Üí	"time"
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/resp"
    10‚Üí)
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üítype SellerAnalyticsAPI struct {
    13‚Üí	DB *pgxpool.Pool
    14‚Üí}
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üítype RevenueByCycle struct {
    17‚Üí	CycleDate   string `json:"cycle_date"`
    18‚Üí	RevenueCents int   `json:"revenue_cents"`
    19‚Üí	Orders       int   `json:"orders"`
    20‚Üí	Pickups      int   `json:"pickups"`
    21‚Üí}
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üítype AnalyticsResponse struct {
    24‚Üí	ActiveSubscribers int `json:"active_subscribers"`
    25‚Üí	TotalSubscribers  int `json:"total_subscribers"`
    26‚Üí	ChurnCount        int `json:"churn_count"`
    27‚Üí	TotalRevenueCents int `json:"total_revenue_cents"`
    28‚Üí	TotalOrders       int `json:"total_orders"`
    29‚Üí	PickedUpCount     int `json:"picked_up_count"`
    30‚Üí	PickupRate        float64          `json:"pickup_rate"`
    31‚Üí	RevenueByCycle    []RevenueByCycle `json:"revenue_by_cycle"`
    32‚Üí}
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üífunc (a SellerAnalyticsAPI) GetAnalytics(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, u AuthUser, sc StoreContext) {
    35‚Üí	ctx := r.Context()
    36‚Üí	var out AnalyticsResponse
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí	// Subscription metrics: join subscription_plans ‚Üí subscriptions.
    39‚Üí	if err := a.DB.QueryRow(ctx, `
    40‚Üí		select
    41‚Üí			coalesce(count(*) filter (where s.status = 'active'), 0)::int,
    42‚Üí			coalesce(count(*), 0)::int,
    43‚Üí			coalesce(count(*) filter (where s.status = 'canceled'), 0)::int
    44‚Üí		from subscriptions s
    45‚Üí		join subscription_plans sp on sp.id = s.plan_id
    46‚Üí		where sp.store_id = $1::uuid
    47‚Üí	`, sc.StoreID).Scan(
    48‚Üí		&out.ActiveSubscribers,
    49‚Üí		&out.TotalSubscribers,
    50‚Üí		&out.ChurnCount,
    51‚Üí	); err != nil {
    52‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
    53‚Üí		return
    54‚Üí	}
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí	// Order-level metrics.
    57‚Üí	if err := a.DB.QueryRow(ctx, `
    58‚Üí		select
    59‚Üí			coalesce(sum(subtotal_cents) filter (where status = 'picked_up'), 0)::int,
    60‚Üí			coalesce(count(*) filter (where status not in ('canceled')), 0)::int,
    61‚Üí			coalesce(count(*) filter (where status = 'picked_up'), 0)::int
    62‚Üí		from orders
    63‚Üí		where store_id = $1::uuid
    64‚Üí	`, sc.StoreID).Scan(
    65‚Üí		&out.TotalRevenueCents,
    66‚Üí		&out.TotalOrders,
    67‚Üí		&out.PickedUpCount,
    68‚Üí	); err != nil {
    69‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
    70‚Üí		return
    71‚Üí	}
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí	if out.TotalOrders > 0 {
    74‚Üí		out.PickupRate = float64(out.PickedUpCount) / float64(out.TotalOrders)
    75‚Üí	}
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí	// Revenue grouped by pickup window date (last 12 cycles).
    78‚Üí	rows, err := a.DB.Query(ctx, `
    79‚Üí		select
    80‚Üí			pw.start_at::date::text as cycle_date,
    81‚Üí			coalesce(sum(o.subtotal_cents) filter (where o.status = 'picked_up'), 0)::int as revenue_cents,
    82‚Üí			count(o.id)::int as orders,
    83‚Üí			coalesce(count(o.id) filter (where o.status = 'picked_up'), 0)::int as pickups
    84‚Üí		from pickup_windows pw
    85‚Üí		join orders o on o.pickup_window_id = pw.id
    86‚Üí		where pw.store_id = $1::uuid
    87‚Üí			and o.status not in ('canceled')
    88‚Üí		group by pw.start_at::date
    89‚Üí		order by pw.start_at::date desc
    90‚Üí		limit 12
    91‚Üí	`, sc.StoreID)
    92‚Üí	if err != nil {
    93‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
    94‚Üí		return
    95‚Üí	}
    96‚Üí	defer rows.Close()
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí	out.RevenueByCycle = make([]RevenueByCycle, 0)
    99‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   100‚Üí		var c RevenueByCycle
   101‚Üí		if err := rows.Scan(&c.CycleDate, &c.RevenueCents, &c.Orders, &c.Pickups); err != nil {
   102‚Üí			resp.Internal(w, err)
   103‚Üí			return
   104‚Üí		}
   105‚Üí		out.RevenueByCycle = append(out.RevenueByCycle, c)
   106‚Üí	}
   107‚Üí	if rows.Err() != nil {
   108‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, rows.Err())
   109‚Üí		return
   110‚Üí	}
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí	// Reverse so oldest first (chronological order for charts).
   113‚Üí	for i, j := 0, len(out.RevenueByCycle)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
   114‚Üí		out.RevenueByCycle[i], out.RevenueByCycle[j] = out.RevenueByCycle[j], out.RevenueByCycle[i]
   115‚Üí	}
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí	resp.OK(w, out)
   118‚Üí}
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí// PayoutHistoryEntry represents a single order's payout info.
   121‚Üítype PayoutHistoryEntry struct {
   122‚Üí	OrderID          string    `json:"order_id"`
   123‚Üí	PickupDate       time.Time `json:"pickup_date"`
   124‚Üí	TotalCents       int       `json:"total_cents"`
   125‚Üí	SellerPayoutCents int      `json:"seller_payout_cents"`
   126‚Üí	PlatformFeeCents int       `json:"platform_fee_cents"`
   127‚Üí	Status           string    `json:"status"`
   128‚Üí	TransferID       *string   `json:"transfer_id"`
   129‚Üí}
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí// GetPayoutHistory lists individual order payouts for a store (orders that reached picked_up or no_show with captured funds).
   132‚Üífunc (a SellerAnalyticsAPI) GetPayoutHistory(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, u AuthUser, sc StoreContext) {
   133‚Üí	ctx := r.Context()
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí	rows, err := a.DB.Query(ctx, `
   136‚Üí		select
   137‚Üí			o.id::text,
   138‚Üí			coalesce(o.picked_up_at, o.updated_at) as payout_date,
   139‚Üí			o.total_cents,
   140‚Üí			o.subtotal_cents as seller_payout_cents,
   141‚Üí			o.buyer_fee_cents as platform_fee_cents,
   142‚Üí			o.status,
   143‚Üí			o.stripe_transfer_id
   144‚Üí		from orders o
   145‚Üí		where o.store_id = $1::uuid
   146‚Üí			and o.status in ('picked_up', 'no_show')
   147‚Üí			and o.captured_cents > 0
   148‚Üí		order by coalesce(o.picked_up_at, o.updated_at) desc
   149‚Üí		limit 50
   150‚Üí	`, sc.StoreID)
   151‚Üí	if err != nil {
   152‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
   153‚Üí		return
   154‚Üí	}
   155‚Üí	defer rows.Close()
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí	out := make([]PayoutHistoryEntry, 0)
   158‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   159‚Üí		var e PayoutHistoryEntry
   160‚Üí		if err := rows.Scan(
   161‚Üí			&e.OrderID,
   162‚Üí			&e.PickupDate,
   163‚Üí			&e.TotalCents,
   164‚Üí			&e.SellerPayoutCents,
   165‚Üí			&e.PlatformFeeCents,
   166‚Üí			&e.Status,
   167‚Üí			&e.TransferID,
   168‚Üí		); err != nil {
   169‚Üí			resp.Internal(w, err)
   170‚Üí			return
   171‚Üí		}
   172‚Üí		out = append(out, e)
   173‚Üí	}
   174‚Üí	if rows.Err() != nil {
   175‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, rows.Err())
   176‚Üí		return
   177‚Üí	}
   178‚Üí
   179‚Üí	resp.OK(w, out)
   180‚Üí}
   181‚Üí

1‚Üípackage v1
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"

    10‚Üí)
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí// transferToSeller transfers funds from a captured payment to the seller's
    13‚Üí// Stripe Connect account. It silently no-ops if the seller has no active
    14‚Üí// Connect account or if the transfer amount is zero.
    15‚Üífunc transferToSeller(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, sc *stripepay.Client, storeID, orderID, piID string, amount int, idempotencyPrefix string) {
    16‚Üí	if amount <= 0 || sc == nil || !sc.Enabled() {
    17‚Üí		return
    18‚Üí	}
    19‚Üí	var connectAccountID *string
    20‚Üí	var connectStatus string
    21‚Üí	if err := db.QueryRow(ctx, `
    22‚Üí		select stripe_account_id, stripe_account_status
    23‚Üí		from stores
    24‚Üí		where id = $1::uuid
    25‚Üí	`, storeID).Scan(&connectAccountID, &connectStatus); err != nil {
    26‚Üí		return
    27‚Üí	}
    28‚Üí	if connectAccountID == nil || strings.TrimSpace(*connectAccountID) == "" || connectStatus != "active" {
    29‚Üí		return
    30‚Üí	}
    31‚Üí	chargeID, _ := sc.GetChargeIDFromPaymentIntent(ctx, piID)
    32‚Üí	transferID, err := sc.CreateTransfer(ctx, amount, *connectAccountID, chargeID, idempotencyPrefix+orderID)
    33‚Üí	if err == nil && transferID != "" {
    34‚Üí		_, _ = db.Exec(ctx, `
    35‚Üí			update orders
    36‚Üí			set stripe_transfer_id = $2, updated_at = now()
    37‚Üí			where id = $1::uuid
    38‚Üí		`, orderID, transferID)
    39‚Üí	}
    40‚Üí}

    61‚Üí	`, orderID).Scan(&info.buyerEmail, &info.boxTitle, &info.pickupCode, &info.totalCents, &info.buyerToken)
    62‚Üí	return info, err == nil
    63‚Üí}

### 2. Cron Jobs Registration Pattern

Location: `/Users/brandonqueener/Cursor Projects/Local-Roots/backend/internal/scheduler/scheduler.go`

**Pattern:**
```go
// Start launches in-process cron jobs. It blocks until ctx is cancelled.
func Start(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, stripeClient *stripepay.Client, emailClient *email.Client, frontendURL string)
```

**Registration mechanism:**
- Uses `time.NewTicker()` with time intervals (e.g., 30 minutes)
- Goroutines spawned with `go runBilling()` and `go runReminders()`
- Recovery deferred in each job function to catch panics
- Jobs run on `case <-ticker.C` in a select loop

**Current jobs:**
1. **Billing Authorization**: Runs every 30 minutes, calls `v1.RunBillingAuthorization()`
2. **Pickup Reminders**: Runs every 30 minutes (staggered 15 min from billing), calls `v1.RunPickupReminders()`

**Job signature pattern:**
```go
func runBilling(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, stripeClient *stripepay.Client) {
    defer func() {
        if r := recover(); r != nil {
            log.Printf("scheduler: billing panic: %v", r)
        }
    }()
    // ... implementation
}
```

---

### 3. Email Templates Pattern

Location: `/Users/brandonqueener/Cursor Projects/Local-Roots/backend/internal/email/templates.go`

**Pattern:** Functions returning (subject, body) tuple of strings

**Template functions (13 total):**
1. `SubscriptionConfirmed(boxTitle, nextPickupDate, location, manageURL)` - Subscription confirmation
2. `PickupReminder(boxTitle, pickupTime, location, pickupCode, orderURL)` - Tomorrow pickup reminder
3. `OrderReady(boxTitle, pickupCode, orderURL)` - Order ready for pickup
4. `MagicLink(verifyURL)` - Buyer magic link sign-in
5. `PaymentReceipt(amountFormatted, boxTitle, orderURL)` - Payment receipt
6. `OneTimeOrderConfirmed(boxTitle, pickupCode, orderURL)` - One-time order confirmation
7. `NewSubscriberNotification(buyerEmail, planTitle, storeName)` - New subscriber alert to seller
8. `NewOrderNotification(buyerEmail, boxTitle, storeName)` - New order alert to seller
9. `NoShowNotification(boxTitle, feeFormatted, orderURL)` - No-show fee charged
10. `NoShowWaived(boxTitle, orderURL)` - No-show fee waived
11. `OrderCanceled(boxTitle, orderURL)` - Order cancellation notification
12. `SubscriptionCanceled(planTitle, storeName)` - Subscription cancellation notification

**Implementation style:**
- Each function uses `fmt.Sprintf()` for templating
- Returns plain text emails (no HTML)
- Simple variable substitution, no complex logic

---

### 4. Endpoint Registration Pattern

Location: `/Users/brandonqueener/Cursor Projects/Local-Roots/backend/internal/httpx/handler.go`

**Function signature:**
```go
func NewHandler(deps Deps) http.Handler
```

**Pattern:**
```go
mux := http.NewServeMux()
mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/stores", public.ListStores)
mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/auth/register", WithRateLimit("auth", authAPI.Register))
mux.HandleFunc("PATCH /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.UpdateStore)))
```

**Registration components:**
- `http.NewServeMux()` for routing
- `METHOD /path/pattern` syntax (Go 1.22+ style)
- **Middleware wrapping patterns:**
  - `authAPI.RequireUser()` - JWT authentication wrapper
  - `v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, handler)` - Store ownership check
  - `WithRateLimit("bucket-name", handler)` - Rate limiting by bucket
- **API groups** (struct-based):
  - `PublicAPI` - unauthenticated endpoints
  - `AuthAPI` - core auth (register/login)
  - `OAuthAPI` - Google OAuth
  - `BuyerAuthAPI` - Buyer magic link auth
  - `BuyerOrdersAPI` - Buyer order queries
  - `OrdersAPI` - Order creation
  - `SellerAPI` - Store/product/window management
  - `SellerOrdersAPI` - Order fulfillment
  - `SellerAnalyticsAPI` - Analytics/payout history
  - And 10+ more specialized APIs

**Return chain:**
```go
return withSecurityHeaders(withLogging(withCORS(deps.Config, mux)))
```

---

### 5. Analytics Response Structure

Location: `/Users/brandonqueener/Cursor Projects/Local-Roots/backend/internal/api/v1/seller_analytics.go`

**AnalyticsResponse struct:**
```go
type AnalyticsResponse struct {
    ActiveSubscribers int              `json:"active_subscribers"`
    TotalSubscribers  int              `json:"total_subscribers"`
    ChurnCount        int              `json:"churn_count"`
    TotalRevenueCents int              `json:"total_revenue_cents"`
    TotalOrders       int              `json:"total_orders"`
    PickedUpCount     int              `json:"picked_up_count"`
    PickupRate        float64          `json:"pickup_rate"`
    RevenueByCycle    []RevenueByCycle `json:"revenue_by_cycle"`
}
```

**RevenueByCycle struct:**
```go
type RevenueByCycle struct {
    CycleDate      string `json:"cycle_date"`
    RevenueCents   int    `json:"revenue_cents"`
    Orders         int    `json:"orders"`
    Pickups        int    `json:"pickups"`
}
```

**Data sources:**
- **Subscription metrics**: Join `subscriptions` and `subscription_plans` tables, filter by store_id
- **Order metrics**: Aggregates from `orders` table (revenue, total, pickup count)
- **Revenue by cycle**: Last 12 pickup windows grouped by `pickup_windows.start_at::date`
- **Pickup rate**: Calculated as `picked_up_count / total_orders`

**Query behavior:**
- Excludes canceled orders (for totals)
- Only counts `picked_up` status for revenue calculations
- Returns RevenueByCycle in reverse order (oldest first for chronological charts)

---

### 6. transferToSeller Function

Location: `/Users/brandonqueener/Cursor Projects/Local-Roots/backend/internal/api/v1/order_helpers.go` (lines 12-40)

**Function signature:**
```go
func transferToSeller(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, sc *stripepay.Client, storeID, orderID, piID string, amount int, idempotencyPrefix string)
```

**What it does:**
1. **Checks prerequisites** - silently no-ops if:
   - Amount <= 0
   - Stripe client is nil or not enabled
2. **Queries seller Connect account** - looks up `stripe_account_id` and `stripe_account_status` from `stores` table
3. **Validates Connect account** - no-ops if:
   - Account ID is nil or empty
   - Account status is not "active"
4. **Retrieves charge ID** - calls `sc.GetChargeIDFromPaymentIntent(piID)`
5. **Creates transfer** - calls `sc.CreateTransfer()` with:
   - Amount in cents
   - Connected account ID
   - Charge ID
   - Idempotent key (prefix + orderID)
6. **Updates order** - on successful transfer, stores `stripe_transfer_id` in `orders` table

**Key design pattern:** Silent failure mode - logs nothing, returns no error. Idempotent via prefix + orderID key.

---

### 7. Additional Helper Function: orderEmailInfo

Also in `order_helpers.go`:
```go
type orderEmailInfo struct {
    buyerEmail string
    boxTitle   string
    pickupCode string
    totalCents int
    buyerToken string
}

func fetchOrderEmailInfo(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool, orderID string) (orderEmailInfo, bool)
```

Fetches order details for email sending (buyer email, product title, pickup code, total, auth token).

|------|-------------------|-----------|
| **Cron jobs** | `scheduler.Start()` ‚Üí ticker.C ‚Üí goroutine | 30-min billing + 30-min reminders (15-min stagger) |
| **Email templates** | Function returning (subject, body) | 13 functions using `fmt.Sprintf()` |
| **Endpoint registration** | `http.NewServeMux()` + `METHOD /path` | Middleware wrapping: RequireUser > RequireStoreOwner > WithRateLimit |
| **Analytics response** | `AnalyticsResponse` struct + SQL aggregation | Subscriptions, orders, revenue, pickup rate, 12-cycle history |
| **transferToSeller** | Silent no-op on invalid conditions | Idempotent Stripe transfer to connected account |
| **Last migration** | **0026** | 0026_box_previews.sql |

**Data sources:**
- **Subscription metrics**: Join `subscriptions` and `subscription_plans` tables, filter by store_id
- **Order metrics**: Aggregates from `orders` table (revenue, total, pickup count)
- **Revenue by cycle**: Last 12 pickup windows grouped by `pickup_windows.start_at::date`
- **Pickup rate**: Calculated as `picked_up_count / total_orders`

**Query behavior:**
- Excludes canceled orders (for totals)
- Only counts `picked_up` status for revenue calculations
- Returns RevenueByCycle in reverse order (oldest first for chronological charts)

---

### 6. transferToSeller Function

Location: `/Users/brandonqueener/Cursor Projects/Local-Roots/backend/internal/api/v1/order_helpers.go` (lines 12-40)

**Function signature:**
```go
func transferToSeller(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, sc *stripepay.Client, storeID, orderID, piID string, amount int, idempotencyPrefix string)
```

**What it does:**
1. **Checks prerequisites** - silently no-ops if:
   - Amount <= 0
   - Stripe client is nil or not enabled
2. **Queries seller Connect account** - looks up `stripe_account_id` and `stripe_account_status` from `stores` table
3. **Validates Connect account** - no-ops if:
   - Account ID is nil or empty
   - Account status is not "active"
4. **Retrieves charge ID** - calls `sc.GetChargeIDFromPaymentIntent(piID)`
5. **Creates transfer** - calls `sc.CreateTransfer()` with:
   - Amount in cents
   - Connected account ID
   - Charge ID
   - Idempotent key (prefix + orderID)
6. **Updates order** - on successful transfer, stores `stripe_transfer_id` in `orders` table

**Key design pattern:** Silent failure mode - logs nothing, returns no error. Idempotent via prefix + orderID key.

---

### 7. Additional Helper Function: orderEmailInfo

Also in `order_helpers.go`:
```go
type orderEmailInfo struct {
    buyerEmail string
    boxTitle   string
    pickupCode string
    totalCents int
    buyerToken string
}

func fetchOrderEmailInfo(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool, orderID string) (orderEmailInfo, bool)
```

Fetches order details for email sending (buyer email, product title, pickup code, total, auth token).

---

## Summary Table

| Item | Pattern / Location | Key Detail |
|------|-------------------|-----------|
| **Cron jobs** | `scheduler.Start()` ‚Üí ticker.C ‚Üí goroutine | 30-min billing + 30-min reminders (15-min stagger) |
| **Email templates** | Function returning (subject, body) | 13 functions using `fmt.Sprintf()` |
| **Endpoint registration** | `http.NewServeMux()` + `METHOD /path` | Middleware wrapping: RequireUser > RequireStoreOwner > WithRateLimit |
| **Analytics response** | `AnalyticsResponse` struct + SQL aggregation | Subscriptions, orders, revenue, pickup rate, 12-cycle history |
| **transferToSeller** | Silent no-op on invalid conditions | Idempotent Stripe transfer to connected account |
| **Last migration** | **0026** | 0026_box_previews.sql |
Important discovery ‚Äî **box previews and location CRUD already exist** in the codebase. Let me investigate what's already built before writing any code.

1‚Üípackage scheduler
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"context"
     5‚Üí	"log"
     6‚Üí	"time"
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí	v1 "github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/api/v1"
    11‚Üí	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/email"
    12‚Üí	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/payments/stripepay"
    13‚Üí)
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí// Start launches in-process cron jobs. It blocks until ctx is cancelled.
    16‚Üí// Call this in a goroutine from main.go.
    17‚Üí//
    18‚Üí// Jobs:
    19‚Üí//   - Billing authorization: every 30 minutes
    20‚Üí//   - Pickup reminders: every 30 minutes (offset by 15 min)
    21‚Üífunc Start(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, stripeClient *stripepay.Client, emailClient *email.Client, frontendURL string) {
    22‚Üí	billingTicker := time.NewTicker(30 * time.Minute)
    23‚Üí	reminderTicker := time.NewTicker(30 * time.Minute)
    24‚Üí	defer billingTicker.Stop()
    25‚Üí	defer reminderTicker.Stop()
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí	log.Printf("scheduler: started (billing=30m, reminders=30m)")
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí	// Run billing immediately on startup, then on tick.
    30‚Üí	go runBilling(ctx, db, stripeClient)
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí	// Delay reminders by 15 min to stagger with billing.
    33‚Üí	go func() {
    34‚Üí		select {
    35‚Üí		case <-time.After(15 * time.Minute):
    36‚Üí			runReminders(ctx, db, emailClient, frontendURL)
    37‚Üí		case <-ctx.Done():
    38‚Üí		}
    39‚Üí	}()
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí	for {
    42‚Üí		select {
    43‚Üí		case <-ctx.Done():
    44‚Üí			log.Printf("scheduler: stopped")
    45‚Üí			return
    46‚Üí		case <-billingTicker.C:
    47‚Üí			go runBilling(ctx, db, stripeClient)
    48‚Üí		case <-reminderTicker.C:
    49‚Üí			go runReminders(ctx, db, emailClient, frontendURL)
    50‚Üí		}
    51‚Üí	}
    52‚Üí}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üífunc runBilling(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, stripeClient *stripepay.Client) {
    55‚Üí	defer func() {
    56‚Üí		if r := recover(); r != nil {
    57‚Üí			log.Printf("scheduler: billing panic: %v", r)
    58‚Üí		}
    59‚Üí	}()
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí	if db == nil || stripeClient == nil || !stripeClient.Enabled() {
    62‚Üí		return
    63‚Üí	}
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí	result, err := v1.RunBillingAuthorization(ctx, db, stripeClient)
    66‚Üí	if err != nil {
    67‚Üí		log.Printf("scheduler: billing error: %v", err)
    68‚Üí		return
    69‚Üí	}
    70‚Üí	log.Printf("scheduler: billing candidates=%d authorized=%d failed=%d until=%s",
    71‚Üí		result.Candidates, result.Authorized, result.Failed, result.Until.Format(time.RFC3339))
    72‚Üí}
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üífunc runReminders(ctx context.Context, db *pgxpool.Pool, emailClient *email.Client, frontendURL string) {
    75‚Üí	defer func() {
    76‚Üí		if r := recover(); r != nil {
    77‚Üí			log.Printf("scheduler: reminders panic: %v", r)
    78‚Üí		}
    79‚Üí	}()
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí	if db == nil || emailClient == nil || !emailClient.Enabled() {
    82‚Üí		return
    83‚Üí	}
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí	result, err := v1.RunPickupReminders(ctx, db, emailClient, frontendURL)
    86‚Üí	if err != nil {
    87‚Üí		log.Printf("scheduler: reminders error: %v", err)
    88‚Üí		return
    89‚Üí	}
    90‚Üí	log.Printf("scheduler: reminders candidates=%d sent=%d",
    91‚Üí		result.Candidates, result.Sent)
    92‚Üí}
    93‚Üí

package email

import "fmt"

func SubscriptionConfirmed(boxTitle string, nextPickupDate string, location string, manageURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("You're subscribed to %s", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

You're now subscribed to %s.

Your next pickup:
  %s
  %s

Manage your subscription:
  %s

Thanks for supporting local food!

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, nextPickupDate, location, manageURL)
	return
}

func PickupReminder(boxTitle string, pickupTime string, location string, pickupCode string, orderURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Pickup reminder: %s tomorrow", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

Your %s pickup is coming up:

  When: %s
  Where: %s
  Pickup code: %s

View your order:
  %s

See you there!

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, pickupTime, location, pickupCode, orderURL)
	return
}

func OrderReady(boxTitle string, pickupCode string, orderURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Your %s order is ready!", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

Your %s order is ready for pickup!

  Pickup code: %s

View your order:
  %s

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, pickupCode, orderURL)
	return
}

func MagicLink(verifyURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = "Your Local Roots sign-in link"
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

Click the link below to sign in to Local Roots:

  %s

This link expires in 15 minutes. If you didn't request this, you can ignore this email.

‚Äî Local Roots
`, verifyURL)
	return
}

func PaymentReceipt(amountFormatted string, boxTitle string, orderURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Receipt: %s for %s", amountFormatted, boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

Your payment of %s for %s has been processed.

View your order:
  %s

Thank you for supporting local food!

‚Äî Local Roots
`, amountFormatted, boxTitle, orderURL)
	return
}

func OneTimeOrderConfirmed(boxTitle, pickupCode, orderURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Order confirmed: %s", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

Your order for %s has been placed.

  Pickup code: %s

View your order:
  %s

Thanks for supporting local food!

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, pickupCode, orderURL)
	return
}

func NewSubscriberNotification(buyerEmail, planTitle, storeName string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("New subscriber to %s", planTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

You have a new subscriber to %s at %s.

  Buyer: %s

Log in to your dashboard to view details.

‚Äî Local Roots
`, planTitle, storeName, buyerEmail)
	return
}

func NewOrderNotification(buyerEmail, boxTitle, storeName string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("New order: %s", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

A new order has been placed for %s at %s.

  Buyer: %s

Log in to your dashboard to view and manage this order.

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, storeName, buyerEmail)
	return
}

func NoShowNotification(boxTitle, feeFormatted, orderURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Missed pickup: %s", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

It looks like you missed your pickup for %s.

A no-show fee of %s has been charged.

View your order:
  %s

If you believe this is an error, please contact the seller.

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, feeFormatted, orderURL)
	return
}

func NoShowWaived(boxTitle, orderURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Missed pickup: %s", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

It looks like you missed your pickup for %s.

The no-show fee has been waived this time.

View your order:
  %s

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, orderURL)
	return
}

func OrderCanceled(boxTitle, orderURL string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Order canceled: %s", boxTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

Your order for %s has been canceled.

View your order:
  %s

If you have any questions, please contact the seller.

‚Äî Local Roots
`, boxTitle, orderURL)
	return
}

func SubscriptionCanceled(planTitle, storeName string) (subject string, body string) {
	subject = fmt.Sprintf("Subscription canceled: %s", planTitle)
	body = fmt.Sprintf(`Hi there!

Your subscription to %s at %s has been canceled.

You will not be charged for future pickups.

If you'd like to resubscribe, visit the store page.

‚Äî Local Roots
`, planTitle, storeName)
	return
}

package httpx

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
	v1 "github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/api/v1"
	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/config"
	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/email"
	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/health"
	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/payments/stripepay"
)

type Deps struct {
	Config config.Config
	DB     *pgxpool.Pool
	Stripe *stripepay.Client
	Email  *email.Client
}

func NewHandler(deps Deps) http.Handler {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	mux.HandleFunc("GET /health", health.Handler(deps.DB))

	// Placeholder for versioned API.
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{"name":"local-roots","env":"` + deps.Config.Env + `"}`))
	})

	stripeClient := deps.Stripe
	emailClient := deps.Email

	public := v1.PublicAPI{DB: deps.DB, JWTSecret: deps.Config.JWTSecret}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/stores", public.ListStores)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/stores/{storeId}", public.GetStore)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/stores/{storeId}/pickup-windows", public.ListStorePickupWindows)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}", public.GetPickupWindow)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}/offerings", public.ListPickupWindowOfferings)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/stores/{storeId}/reviews", public.ListStoreReviews)

	sub := v1.SubscriptionAPI{
		DB:              deps.DB,
		Stripe:          stripeClient,
		BuyerFeeBps:     deps.Config.BuyerFeeBps,
		BuyerFeeFlatCts: deps.Config.BuyerFeeFlatCents,
		Email:           emailClient,
		FrontendURL:     deps.Config.FrontendURL,
	}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/stores/{storeId}/subscription-plans", sub.ListStorePlans)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/subscription-plans/{planId}", sub.GetPlan)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/subscription-plans/{planId}/checkout", WithRateLimit("checkout", sub.Checkout))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/subscription-plans/{planId}/subscribe", WithRateLimit("checkout", sub.Subscribe))

	orders := v1.OrdersAPI{
		DB:              deps.DB,
		Stripe:          stripeClient,
		BuyerFeeBps:     deps.Config.BuyerFeeBps,
		BuyerFeeFlatCts: deps.Config.BuyerFeeFlatCents,
		Email:           emailClient,
		FrontendURL:     deps.Config.FrontendURL,
	}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}/orders", WithRateLimit("checkout", orders.CreateOrder))

	orderCheckout := v1.OrderCheckoutAPI{
		DB:              deps.DB,
		Stripe:          stripeClient,
		BuyerFeeBps:     deps.Config.BuyerFeeBps,
		BuyerFeeFlatCts: deps.Config.BuyerFeeFlatCents,
	}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}/checkout", WithRateLimit("checkout", orderCheckout.Checkout))

	buyerOrders := v1.BuyerOrdersAPI{DB: deps.DB, JWTSecret: deps.Config.JWTSecret}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/orders/{orderId}", buyerOrders.GetOrder)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/orders/{orderId}/review", buyerOrders.CreateReview)

	buyerSubs := v1.BuyerSubscriptionsAPI{DB: deps.DB, Stripe: stripeClient, JWTSecret: deps.Config.JWTSecret, Email: emailClient, FrontendURL: deps.Config.FrontendURL}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}", buyerSubs.GetSubscription)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/status", buyerSubs.UpdateStatus)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/payment-method/setup", buyerSubs.SetupPaymentMethod)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/payment-method/confirm", buyerSubs.ConfirmPaymentMethod)

	stripeWebhook := v1.StripeWebhookAPI{DB: deps.DB, WebhookSecret: deps.Config.StripeWebhookSecret}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/stripe/webhook", WithRateLimit("webhook", stripeWebhook.StripeWebhook))

	authAPI := v1.AuthAPI{DB: deps.DB, JWTSecret: deps.Config.JWTSecret}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/auth/register", WithRateLimit("auth", authAPI.Register))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/auth/login", WithRateLimit("auth", authAPI.Login))

	oauthAPI := v1.OAuthAPI{DB: deps.DB, JWTSecret: deps.Config.JWTSecret, GoogleOAuthClientID: deps.Config.GoogleOAuthClientID}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/auth/google", WithRateLimit("auth", oauthAPI.GoogleLogin))

	buyerAuthAPI := v1.BuyerAuthAPI{DB: deps.DB, JWTSecret: deps.Config.JWTSecret, Email: emailClient, FrontendURL: deps.Config.FrontendURL}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/buyer/auth/magic-link", WithRateLimit("auth", buyerAuthAPI.SendMagicLink))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/buyer/auth/verify", WithRateLimit("auth", buyerAuthAPI.Verify))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/buyer/me", authAPI.RequireUser(buyerAuthAPI.GetMe))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/buyer/orders", authAPI.RequireUser(buyerAuthAPI.ListOrders))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/buyer/subscriptions", authAPI.RequireUser(buyerAuthAPI.ListSubscriptions))

	seller := v1.SellerAPI{DB: deps.DB}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores", authAPI.RequireUser(seller.ListMyStores))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores", authAPI.RequireUser(seller.CreateStore))
	mux.HandleFunc("PATCH /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.UpdateStore)))
	mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.DeleteStore)))

	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-locations", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.ListPickupLocations)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-locations", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.CreatePickupLocation)))
	mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-locations/{pickupLocationId}", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.DeletePickupLocation)))
	mux.HandleFunc("PATCH /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-locations/{pickupLocationId}", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.UpdatePickupLocation)))

	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-windows", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.ListPickupWindows)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-windows", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.CreatePickupWindow)))

	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/products", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.ListProducts)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/products", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.CreateProduct)))

	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}/offerings", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.ListOfferings)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}/offerings", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.CreateOffering)))

	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/subscriptions", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.ListSubscriptions)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/cancel", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, seller.CancelSubscription)))

	sellerOrders := v1.SellerOrdersAPI{DB: deps.DB, Stripe: stripeClient, NoShowFeeCents: deps.Config.NoShowFeeCents, NoShowPlatformSplitBps: deps.Config.NoShowPlatformSplitBps, Email: emailClient, FrontendURL: deps.Config.FrontendURL}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}/orders", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerOrders.ListOrdersForPickupWindow)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/orders/{orderId}/status", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerOrders.UpdateOrderStatus)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/orders/{orderId}/confirm-pickup", WithRateLimit("pickup", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerOrders.ConfirmPickup))))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/orders/lookup-by-code", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerOrders.LookupByCode)))

	pickupConfirm := v1.PickupConfirmAPI{DB: deps.DB, Stripe: stripeClient, Email: emailClient, FrontendURL: deps.Config.FrontendURL}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/pickup/preview", authAPI.RequireUser(pickupConfirm.Preview))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/pickup/confirm", WithRateLimit("pickup", authAPI.RequireUser(pickupConfirm.Confirm)))

	sellerPayouts := v1.SellerPayoutsAPI{DB: deps.DB, NoShowPlatformSplitBps: deps.Config.NoShowPlatformSplitBps}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/pickup-windows/{pickupWindowId}/payout-summary", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerPayouts.GetPickupWindowPayoutSummary)))

	sellerAnalytics := v1.SellerAnalyticsAPI{DB: deps.DB}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/analytics", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerAnalytics.GetAnalytics)))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/payouts", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerAnalytics.GetPayoutHistory)))

	sellerConnect := v1.SellerConnectAPI{DB: deps.DB, Stripe: stripeClient, FrontendURL: deps.Config.FrontendURL}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/connect/onboard", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerConnect.Onboard)))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/connect/status", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerConnect.GetStatus)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/connect/refresh-link", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerConnect.RefreshLink)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/connect/account-session", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerConnect.AccountSession)))

	boxPreviews := v1.BoxPreviewAPI{DB: deps.DB}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/plans/{planId}/previews", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, boxPreviews.Create)))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/plans/{planId}/previews", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, boxPreviews.List)))
	mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/plans/{planId}/previews/{previewId}", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, boxPreviews.Delete)))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/plans/{planId}/preview/latest", boxPreviews.Latest)

	sellerSub := v1.SellerSubscriptionAPI{
		DB:              deps.DB,
		Stripe:          stripeClient,
		BuyerFeeBps:     deps.Config.BuyerFeeBps,
		BuyerFeeFlatCts: deps.Config.BuyerFeeFlatCents,
	}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/subscription-plans", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerSub.ListPlans)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/subscription-plans", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerSub.CreatePlan)))
	mux.HandleFunc("PATCH /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/subscription-plans/{planId}", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerSub.UpdatePlan)))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/stores/{storeId}/subscription-plans/{planId}/generate-cycle", authAPI.RequireUser(v1.RequireStoreOwner(deps.DB, sellerSub.GenerateNextCycle)))

	geo := v1.GeoAPI{GooglePlacesAPIKey: deps.Config.GooglePlacesAPIKey}
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/places/autocomplete", WithRateLimit("geo", geo.PublicAutocomplete))
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v1/geocode", WithRateLimit("geo", geo.PublicGeocode))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/geo/places/autocomplete", authAPI.RequireUser(geo.PlacesAutocomplete))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/geo/places/details", authAPI.RequireUser(geo.PlacesDetails))
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/seller/geo/timezone", authAPI.RequireUser(geo.Timezone))

	internalBilling := v1.InternalBillingAPI{
		DB:     deps.DB,
		Stripe: stripeClient,
		Secret: deps.Config.InternalCronSecret,
	}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/internal/billing/authorize-pending", internalBilling.AuthorizePending)

	internalEmail := v1.InternalEmailAPI{
		DB:          deps.DB,
		Email:       emailClient,
		Secret:      deps.Config.InternalCronSecret,
		FrontendURL: deps.Config.FrontendURL,
	}
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/internal/email/pickup-reminders", internalEmail.SendPickupReminders)

	return withSecurityHeaders(withLogging(withCORS(deps.Config, mux)))
}

1‚Üípackage v1
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üíimport (
     4‚Üí	"net/http"
     5‚Üí	"strconv"
     6‚Üí	"strings"
     7‚Üí	"time"
     8‚Üí
     9‚Üí	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"
    10‚Üí	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/auth"
    13‚Üí	"github.com/paretoimproved/local-roots/backend/internal/resp"
    14‚Üí)
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üítype PublicAPI struct {
    17‚Üí	DB        *pgxpool.Pool
    18‚Üí	JWTSecret string
    19‚Üí}
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üítype Store struct {
    22‚Üí	ID          string    `json:"id"`
    23‚Üí	Name        string    `json:"name"`
    24‚Üí	Description *string   `json:"description"`
    25‚Üí	ImageURL    *string   `json:"image_url,omitempty"`
    26‚Üí	CreatedAt   time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    27‚Üí	City        *string   `json:"city,omitempty"`
    28‚Üí	Region      *string   `json:"region,omitempty"`
    29‚Üí	DistanceKM  *float64  `json:"distance_km,omitempty"`
    30‚Üí	IsDemo      bool      `json:"is_demo"`
    31‚Üí}
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üífunc (a PublicAPI) GetStore(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    34‚Üí	if a.DB == nil {
    35‚Üí		resp.ServiceUnavailable(w, "database not configured")
    36‚Üí		return
    37‚Üí	}
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí	storeID := r.PathValue("storeId")
    40‚Üí	if storeID == "" {
    41‚Üí		resp.BadRequest(w, "missing storeId")
    42‚Üí		return
    43‚Üí	}
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí	var s Store
    46‚Üí	err := a.DB.QueryRow(r.Context(), `
    47‚Üí		select s.id::text, s.name, s.description, s.image_url, s.created_at,
    48‚Üí			nearest.city, nearest.region, s.is_demo
    49‚Üí		from stores s
    50‚Üí		left join lateral (
    51‚Üí			select pl.city, pl.region
    52‚Üí			from pickup_locations pl
    53‚Üí			where pl.store_id = s.id
    54‚Üí			limit 1
    55‚Üí		) nearest on true
    56‚Üí		where s.id = $1::uuid
    57‚Üí			and s.is_active = true
    58‚Üí	`, storeID).Scan(&s.ID, &s.Name, &s.Description, &s.ImageURL, &s.CreatedAt, &s.City, &s.Region, &s.IsDemo)
    59‚Üí	if err != nil {
    60‚Üí		if err == pgx.ErrNoRows {
    61‚Üí			resp.NotFound(w, "store not found")
    62‚Üí			return
    63‚Üí		}
    64‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
    65‚Üí		return
    66‚Üí	}
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí	resp.OK(w, s)
    69‚Üí}
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üífunc (a PublicAPI) ListStores(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    72‚Üí	if a.DB == nil {
    73‚Üí		resp.ServiceUnavailable(w, "database not configured")
    74‚Üí		return
    75‚Üí	}
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí	q := r.URL.Query()
    78‚Üí	showDemo := q.Get("demo") == "true"
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí	// Only honour demo=true for authenticated admin users.
    81‚Üí	if showDemo && a.JWTSecret != "" {
    82‚Üí		allowed := false
    83‚Üí		if hdr := r.Header.Get("Authorization"); strings.HasPrefix(hdr, "Bearer ") {
    84‚Üí			tokenStr := strings.TrimPrefix(hdr, "Bearer ")
    85‚Üí			if claims, err := auth.ParseJWT([]byte(a.JWTSecret), tokenStr); err == nil && claims.Role == "admin" {
    86‚Üí				allowed = true
    87‚Üí			}
    88‚Üí		}
    89‚Üí		if !allowed {
    90‚Üí			showDemo = false
    91‚Üí		}
    92‚Üí	}
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí	latStr := q.Get("lat")
    95‚Üí	lngStr := q.Get("lng")
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí	var hasGeo bool
    98‚Üí	var lat, lng, radiusKM float64
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí	if latStr != "" && lngStr != "" {
   101‚Üí		var err error
   102‚Üí		lat, err = strconv.ParseFloat(latStr, 64)
   103‚Üí		if err != nil || lat < -90 || lat > 90 {
   104‚Üí			resp.BadRequest(w, "invalid lat")
   105‚Üí			return
   106‚Üí		}
   107‚Üí		lng, err = strconv.ParseFloat(lngStr, 64)
   108‚Üí		if err != nil || lng < -180 || lng > 180 {
   109‚Üí			resp.BadRequest(w, "invalid lng")
   110‚Üí			return
   111‚Üí		}
   112‚Üí		hasGeo = true
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí		radiusKM = 80
   115‚Üí		if rStr := q.Get("radius_km"); rStr != "" {
   116‚Üí			radiusKM, err = strconv.ParseFloat(rStr, 64)
   117‚Üí			if err != nil || radiusKM <= 0 {
   118‚Üí				resp.BadRequest(w, "invalid radius_km")
   119‚Üí				return
   120‚Üí			}
   121‚Üí			if radiusKM > 200 {
   122‚Üí				radiusKM = 200
   123‚Üí			}
   124‚Üí		}
   125‚Üí	}
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí	var query string
   128‚Üí	var args []any
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí	args = append(args, showDemo)
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí	if hasGeo {
   133‚Üí		args = append(args, lat, lng, radiusKM)
   134‚Üí		query = `
   135‚Üí			with distances as (
   136‚Üí				select distinct on (s.id)
   137‚Üí					s.id::text, s.name, s.description, s.image_url, s.created_at,
   138‚Üí					pl.city, pl.region,
   139‚Üí					6371 * acos(
   140‚Üí						cos(radians($2)) * cos(radians(pl.lat)) *
   141‚Üí						cos(radians(pl.lng) - radians($3)) +
   142‚Üí						sin(radians($2)) * sin(radians(pl.lat))
   143‚Üí					) as distance_km,
   144‚Üí					s.is_demo
   145‚Üí				from stores s
   146‚Üí				join pickup_locations pl on pl.store_id = s.id
   147‚Üí				where s.is_active = true
   148‚Üí					and ($1 = false or s.is_demo = true)
   149‚Üí					and exists (
   150‚Üí						select 1 from subscription_plans sp
   151‚Üí						where sp.store_id = s.id
   152‚Üí							and sp.is_active = true
   153‚Üí							and sp.is_live = true
   154‚Üí					)
   155‚Üí				order by s.id, distance_km asc
   156‚Üí			)
   157‚Üí			select * from distances
   158‚Üí			where distance_km <= $4
   159‚Üí			order by distance_km asc
   160‚Üí			limit 100
   161‚Üí		`
   162‚Üí	} else {
   163‚Üí		query = `
   164‚Üí			select s.id::text, s.name, s.description, s.image_url, s.created_at,
   165‚Üí				nearest.city, nearest.region, s.is_demo
   166‚Üí			from stores s
   167‚Üí			left join lateral (
   168‚Üí				select pl.city, pl.region
   169‚Üí				from pickup_locations pl
   170‚Üí				where pl.store_id = s.id
   171‚Üí				limit 1
   172‚Üí			) nearest on true
   173‚Üí			where s.is_active = true
   174‚Üí				and ($1 = false or s.is_demo = true)
   175‚Üí				and exists (
   176‚Üí					select 1 from subscription_plans sp
   177‚Üí					where sp.store_id = s.id
   178‚Üí						and sp.is_active = true
   179‚Üí						and sp.is_live = true
   180‚Üí				)
   181‚Üí			order by s.created_at asc
   182‚Üí			limit 100
   183‚Üí		`
   184‚Üí	}
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üí	rows, err := a.DB.Query(r.Context(), query, args...)
   187‚Üí	if err != nil {
   188‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
   189‚Üí		return
   190‚Üí	}
   191‚Üí	defer rows.Close()
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí	out := make([]Store, 0)
   194‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   195‚Üí		var s Store
   196‚Üí		if hasGeo {
   197‚Üí			if err := rows.Scan(&s.ID, &s.Name, &s.Description, &s.ImageURL, &s.CreatedAt, &s.City, &s.Region, &s.DistanceKM, &s.IsDemo); err != nil {
   198‚Üí				resp.Internal(w, err)
   199‚Üí				return
   200‚Üí			}
   201‚Üí		} else {
   202‚Üí			if err := rows.Scan(&s.ID, &s.Name, &s.Description, &s.ImageURL, &s.CreatedAt, &s.City, &s.Region, &s.IsDemo); err != nil {
   203‚Üí				resp.Internal(w, err)
   204‚Üí				return
   205‚Üí			}
   206‚Üí		}
   207‚Üí		out = append(out, s)
   208‚Üí	}
   209‚Üí	if rows.Err() != nil {
   210‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, rows.Err())
   211‚Üí		return
   212‚Üí	}
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí	resp.OK(w, out)
   215‚Üí}
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üítype PickupWindow struct {
   218‚Üí	ID             string    `json:"id"`
   219‚Üí	StartAt        time.Time `json:"start_at"`
   220‚Üí	EndAt          time.Time `json:"end_at"`
   221‚Üí	CutoffAt       time.Time `json:"cutoff_at"`
   222‚Üí	Status         string    `json:"status"`
   223‚Üí	PickupLocation struct {
   224‚Üí		ID           string   `json:"id"`
   225‚Üí		Label        *string  `json:"label"`
   226‚Üí		Address1     string   `json:"address1"`
   227‚Üí		City         string   `json:"city"`
   228‚Üí		Region       string   `json:"region"`
   229‚Üí		Postal       string   `json:"postal_code"`
   230‚Üí		Timezone     string   `json:"timezone"`
   231‚Üí		Lat          *float64 `json:"lat"`
   232‚Üí		Lng          *float64 `json:"lng"`
   233‚Üí		Instructions *string  `json:"instructions"`
   234‚Üí		PhotoURL     *string  `json:"photo_url,omitempty"`
   235‚Üí	} `json:"pickup_location"`
   236‚Üí}
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üífunc (a PublicAPI) ListStorePickupWindows(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   239‚Üí	if a.DB == nil {
   240‚Üí		resp.ServiceUnavailable(w, "database not configured")
   241‚Üí		return
   242‚Üí	}
   243‚Üí
   244‚Üí	storeID := r.PathValue("storeId")
   245‚Üí	if storeID == "" {
   246‚Üí		resp.BadRequest(w, "missing storeId")
   247‚Üí		return
   248‚Üí	}
   249‚Üí
   250‚Üí	status := r.URL.Query().Get("status")
   251‚Üí	if status == "" {
   252‚Üí		status = "published"
   253‚Üí	}
   254‚Üí
   255‚Üí	from := time.Now().UTC()
   256‚Üí	if q := r.URL.Query().Get("from"); q != "" {
   257‚Üí		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, q)
   258‚Üí		if err != nil {
   259‚Üí			resp.BadRequest(w, "invalid from (expected RFC3339)")
   260‚Üí			return
   261‚Üí		}
   262‚Üí		from = t
   263‚Üí	}
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí	rows, err := a.DB.Query(r.Context(), `
   266‚Üí		select
   267‚Üí			pw.id::text,
   268‚Üí			pw.start_at,
   269‚Üí			pw.end_at,
   270‚Üí			pw.cutoff_at,
   271‚Üí			pw.status,
   272‚Üí			pl.id::text,
   273‚Üí			pl.label,
   274‚Üí			pl.address1,
   275‚Üí			pl.city,
   276‚Üí			pl.region,
   277‚Üí			pl.postal_code,
   278‚Üí			pl.timezone,
   279‚Üí			pl.lat,
   280‚Üí			pl.lng,
   281‚Üí			pl.instructions,
   282‚Üí			pl.photo_url
   283‚Üí		from pickup_windows pw
   284‚Üí		join pickup_locations pl on pl.id = pw.pickup_location_id
   285‚Üí		where pw.store_id = $1::uuid
   286‚Üí			and pw.status = $2
   287‚Üí			and pw.start_at >= $3
   288‚Üí		order by pw.start_at asc
   289‚Üí		limit 50
   290‚Üí	`, storeID, status, from)
   291‚Üí	if err != nil {
   292‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
   293‚Üí		return
   294‚Üí	}
   295‚Üí	defer rows.Close()
   296‚Üí
   297‚Üí	out := make([]PickupWindow, 0)
   298‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   299‚Üí		var pw PickupWindow
   300‚Üí		if err := rows.Scan(
   301‚Üí			&pw.ID,
   302‚Üí			&pw.StartAt,
   303‚Üí			&pw.EndAt,
   304‚Üí			&pw.CutoffAt,
   305‚Üí			&pw.Status,
   306‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.ID,
   307‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Label,
   308‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Address1,
   309‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.City,
   310‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Region,
   311‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Postal,
   312‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Timezone,
   313‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Lat,
   314‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Lng,
   315‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.Instructions,
   316‚Üí			&pw.PickupLocation.PhotoURL,
   317‚Üí		); err != nil {
   318‚Üí			resp.Internal(w, err)
   319‚Üí			return
   320‚Üí		}
   321‚Üí		out = append(out, pw)
   322‚Üí	}
   323‚Üí	if rows.Err() != nil {
   324‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, rows.Err())
   325‚Üí		return
   326‚Üí	}
   327‚Üí
   328‚Üí	resp.OK(w, out)
   329‚Üí}

   332‚Üí	ID        string    `json:"id"`

   421‚Üífunc (a PublicAPI) ListPickupWindowOfferings(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   422‚Üí	if a.DB == nil {
   423‚Üí		resp.ServiceUnavailable(w, "database not configured")
   424‚Üí		return
   425‚Üí	}
   426‚Üí
   427‚Üí	windowID := r.PathValue("pickupWindowId")
   428‚Üí	if windowID == "" {
   429‚Üí		resp.BadRequest(w, "missing pickupWindowId")
   430‚Üí		return
   431‚Üí	}
   432‚Üí
   433‚Üí	rows, err := a.DB.Query(r.Context(), `
   434‚Üí		select
   435‚Üí			o.id::text,
   436‚Üí			o.price_cents,
   437‚Üí			o.quantity_available,
   438‚Üí			o.quantity_reserved,
   439‚Üí			o.status,
   440‚Üí			p.id::text,
   441‚Üí			p.title,
   442‚Üí			p.unit,
   443‚Üí			p.description,
   444‚Üí			pi.url
   445‚Üí		from offerings o
   446‚Üí		join products p on p.id = o.product_id
   447‚Üí		left join lateral (
   448‚Üí			select pimg.url
   449‚Üí			from product_images pimg
   450‚Üí			where pimg.product_id = p.id
   451‚Üí			order by pimg.sort_order asc
   452‚Üí			limit 1
   453‚Üí		) pi on true
   454‚Üí		where o.pickup_window_id = $1::uuid
   455‚Üí			and o.status = 'active'
   456‚Üí		order by p.title asc
   457‚Üí		limit 200
   458‚Üí	`, windowID)
   459‚Üí	if err != nil {
   460‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
   461‚Üí		return
   462‚Üí	}
   463‚Üí	defer rows.Close()
   464‚Üí
   465‚Üí	out := make([]Offering, 0)
   466‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   467‚Üí		var o Offering
   468‚Üí		if err := rows.Scan(
   469‚Üí			&o.ID,
   470‚Üí			&o.PriceCents,
   471‚Üí			&o.QuantityAvail,
   472‚Üí			&o.QuantityReserve,
   473‚Üí			&o.Status,
   474‚Üí			&o.Product.ID,
   475‚Üí			&o.Product.Title,
   476‚Üí			&o.Product.Unit,
   477‚Üí			&o.Product.Description,
   478‚Üí			&o.Product.ImageURL,
   479‚Üí		); err != nil {
   480‚Üí			resp.Internal(w, err)
   481‚Üí			return
   482‚Üí		}
   483‚Üí		o.QuantityRemain = o.QuantityAvail - o.QuantityReserve
   484‚Üí		if o.QuantityRemain < 0 {
   485‚Üí			o.QuantityRemain = 0
   486‚Üí		}
   487‚Üí		out = append(out, o)
   488‚Üí	}
   489‚Üí	if rows.Err() != nil {
   490‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, rows.Err())
   491‚Üí		return
   492‚Üí	}
   493‚Üí
   494‚Üí	resp.OK(w, out)
   495‚Üí}

   497‚Üítype PublicReview struct {
   498‚Üí	ID        string    `json:"id"`
   499‚Üí	Rating    int       `json:"rating"`
   500‚Üí	Body      *string   `json:"body"`
   501‚Üí	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
   502‚Üí}
   503‚Üí
   504‚Üítype ReviewsResponse struct {
   505‚Üí	AvgRating   float64        `json:"avg_rating"`
   506‚Üí	ReviewCount int            `json:"review_count"`
   507‚Üí	Reviews     []PublicReview  `json:"reviews"`
   508‚Üí}
   509‚Üí
   510‚Üífunc (a PublicAPI) ListStoreReviews(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   511‚Üí	if a.DB == nil {
   512‚Üí		resp.ServiceUnavailable(w, "database not configured")
   513‚Üí		return
   514‚Üí	}
   515‚Üí
   516‚Üí	storeID := r.PathValue("storeId")
   517‚Üí	if storeID == "" {
   518‚Üí		resp.BadRequest(w, "missing storeId")
   519‚Üí		return
   520‚Üí	}
   521‚Üí
   522‚Üí	rows, err := a.DB.Query(r.Context(), `
   523‚Üí		select id::text, rating, body, created_at,
   524‚Üí			count(*) over() as review_count,
   525‚Üí			coalesce(avg(rating) over()::numeric(2,1), 0) as avg_rating
   526‚Üí		from reviews
   527‚Üí		where store_id = $1::uuid
   528‚Üí		order by created_at desc
   529‚Üí		limit 20
   530‚Üí	`, storeID)
   531‚Üí	if err != nil {
   532‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, err)
   533‚Üí		return
   534‚Üí	}
   535‚Üí	defer rows.Close()
   536‚Üí
   537‚Üí	var avgRating float64
   538‚Üí	var reviewCount int
   539‚Üí	reviews := make([]PublicReview, 0)
   540‚Üí	for rows.Next() {
   541‚Üí		var rev PublicReview
   542‚Üí		if err := rows.Scan(&rev.ID, &rev.Rating, &rev.Body, &rev.CreatedAt, &reviewCount, &avgRating); err != nil {
   543‚Üí			resp.Internal(w, err)
   544‚Üí			return
   545‚Üí		}
   546‚Üí		reviews = append(reviews, rev)
   547‚Üí	}
   548‚Üí	if rows.Err() != nil {
   549‚Üí		resp.Internal(w, rows.Err())
   550‚Üí		return
   551‚Üí	}
   552‚Üí
   553‚Üí	resp.OK(w, ReviewsResponse{
   554‚Üí		AvgRating:   avgRating,
   555‚Üí		ReviewCount: reviewCount,
   556‚Üí		Reviews:     reviews,
   557‚Üí	})
   558‚Üí}
